title: The Tenant Who Never Existed
idea: I recently bought a house built in the 1970s and discovered a hidden, floor-to-ceiling cabinet behind a bedroom mirror. Inside, I found a stack of Polaroids showing the same man sitting in the exact same chair in my living room, but with different furniture and wallpaper styles spanning 40 years. The final photo in the stack shows the man sitting in that chair, looking directly at the camera, holding a modern iPhone—which didn't exist when the photo appears to have been developed. I need the internet to help me identify the house's original blueprint to see if this 'hidden room' was ever legally permitted.
hook: Do not buy a house until you check behind the master bedroom mirror, because I just found out I’m not the only one living here.
targetAudience: True crime enthusiasts, urban explorers, and Reddit-style mystery solvers.
viralAngle: It combines a relatable 'homeowner' fear with a visual puzzle. Viewers will pause to analyze the photos for clues about the timeline and debate in the comments if the iPhone in the photo is a glitch, a prank, or a time-travel paradox, driving massive rewatchability.
